title: cyperus erectus subsp. albescens
intro: Cyperus erectus subsp. albescens, also known as white papyrus sedge, is a plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, due to its expectorant, demulcent, and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation of Cyperus erectus subsp. albescens typically involves drying the leaves and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
